summary: Tested iteration 21 features: Agent command approval with write permission, deployment simulation mode, browser extension download, and PC agent detection classes. All 25 backend tests passed. Frontend Swarm Dashboard loads correctly with all tabs (Setup Scanner, Devices, Telemetry, Deployments).
passed_tests: POST /api/agent-commands/create - Creates command with write permission (returns command_id, status: pending_approval)
POST /api/agent-commands/{id}/approve - Approves command with write permission (not manage_users)
POST /api/agent-commands/{id}/approve - Creates command_queue entry for agent pickup
POST /api/agent-commands/{id}/approve - Rejects command correctly (status: rejected)
GET /api/agent-commands/pending - Returns pending commands list
GET /api/agent-commands/types - Returns all command types (block_ip, kill_process, quarantine_file, full_scan, collect_forensics)
GET /api/swarm/agent/download/browser-extension - Downloads zip file (application/zip)
Browser extension zip contains: manifest.json, background.js, content.js, popup.html, popup.js, blocked.html
manifest.json has manifest_version 3, correct permissions (webRequest, webNavigation, tabs, storage)
background.js has threat detection (maliciousDomains, checkUrl, levenshteinDistance, cryptojackingScripts)
content.js has XSS, phishing, cryptojacking, keylogger detection
seraph_defender_v7.py exists with all detection classes
RootkitDetector class exists with scan methods
HiddenFolderDetector class exists with hidden folder detection
AdminPrivilegesMonitor class exists with get_current_admins method
AliasDetector class exists with suspicious alias patterns
FileIndexer class exists with index_directory and get_file_telemetry methods
All detection classes are instantiated (rootkit_detector, hidden_folder_detector, admin_monitor, alias_detector, file_indexer)
Deployment service has simulation mode for demo (no credentials)
GET /api/swarm/deployment/status - Returns deployment tasks
Agent dashboard HTML has tabs: File Index, Admin Privileges, Rootkit, Hidden Folders, Aliases
Dashboard has file_telemetry panel
Dashboard has admin_info panel
popup.html has stats display (pagesScanned, threatsBlocked, phishingBlocked, malwareBlocked)
blocked.html exists with proper content

deployment.simulation_mode: IMPLEMENTED - simulates success when no credentials provided

browser_extension_files.manifest.json: Manifest v3, permissions: webRequest, webNavigation, tabs, storage, notifications, alarms
browser_extension_files.background.js: Threat detection, URL checking, typosquatting detection, cryptojacking blocking
browser_extension_files.content.js: XSS detection, phishing detection, cryptojacking detection, keylogger detection, data exfiltration monitoring
browser_extension_files.popup.html: Stats display, protection toggles, settings button
browser_extension_files.popup.js: Stats update, toggle handlers, settings management
browser_extension_files.blocked.html: Blocked page with threat reason display

seed_data_creation: Created test commands during testing (cleaned up via approval/rejection)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 21 features are working: Agent command approval uses 'write' permission (fixed from 'manage_users'), deployment service has simulation mode for demo, browser extension download returns zip with all required files (manifest.json, background.js, content.js, popup.html, popup.js, blocked.html), PC agent (seraph_defender_v7.py) has 5 new detection classes (RootkitDetector, HiddenFolderDetector, AdminPrivilegesMonitor, AliasDetector, FileIndexer) all instantiated and integrated into dashboard. Swarm Dashboard has tabs: Setup Scanner, Devices, Telemetry, Deployments.

mocked_api.deployment_simulation: Deployment service simulates success when no real credentials provided (is_simulation flag)
